title: Aromatic Amines Group - Canada.ca
content:
## Aromatic Amines Group
- Aromatic Amines Group
These substances were identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP). This screening assessment focuses on 8 of 15 substances referred to collectively as the Aromatic Amines Group.
- Summary of publications
Draft screening assessment

Proposed conclusion on section 64 criteria

Footnote a This substance was not identified under subsection 73(1) of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 but was included in this assessment as it was considered a priority on the basis of other human health concerns. Return to footnote a referrer
This substance was not identified under subsection 73(1) of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 but was included in this assessment as it was considered a priority on the basis of other human health concerns.
Return to footnote a referrer
Anticipated publication of the Final Screening Assessment for the Aromatic Amines Group and of the Proposed Risk Management Approach for the Aromatic Amines Group, specifically: Benzenamine, N,N-dimethyl- (dimethylaniline) .
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the Draft Screening Assessment for the Aromatic Amines Group (including follow-up activities for NDPhA, 2-aminophenol and MBOCA) and the Risk Management Scope for the Aromatic Amines Group, specifically: Benzenamine, N,N-dimethyl- (dimethylaniline) . The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 154, No. 33 .
Seven other substances were identified as being part of the Aromatic Amines Group at the outset of the third phase of the CMP . These substances were subsequently determined to be of low concern to both human health and the environment through other approaches. Conclusions for CAS RNs 793-24-8, 3081-14-9 and 5285-60-9 are provided in the Final Screening Assessment for Substances Identified as Being of Low Concern using the Ecological Risk Classification of Organic Substances and the Threshold of Toxicological Concern (TTC)-based Approach for Certain Substances . Conclusions for CAS RNs 91-66-7, 95-54-5, 134-09-8 and 13680-35-8 are provided in the Final Screening Assessment for the Rapid Screening of Substances with Limited General Population Exposure .
